revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大井神社 |画像=[[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[笠間市]]大渕字輪台1652 |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 24 | 緯度秒 = 4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 16 | 経度秒 = 13 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = [[神八井耳命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 太郎明神、一の宮明神（四所一宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[11月12日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Ibaraki }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
鳥居
]] '''大井神社'''（おおいじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[笠間市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *'''[[神八井耳命]]''' === 祭神について === 祭神の神八井耳命は、初代[[神武天皇]]の皇子で、二代[[綏靖天皇]]の兄にあたる人物である。『[[日本書紀]]』では、神八井耳命について多臣（[[多氏]]）の祖と記され、『[[古事記]]』では、意富臣・小子部連・坂合部連・火君・大分君・阿蘇君・筑紫三家連・雀部臣・雀部造・小長谷造・都祁直・伊余国造・科野国造・道奥石城国造・常道仲国造・長狭国造・伊勢船木直・尾張丹羽臣・嶋田臣ら19氏の祖と記される。 このうち、当社付近は[[仲国造|常道仲国造]]が治めた地域とされており、仲国造が祖先にあたる神八井耳命を祀ったものと考えられる。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[大同]]元年（[[806年]]）に勅宣により常陸国那珂郡大井村和体ケ峯に社殿を造営し、大井大明神と称したとする。 === 概史 === 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「常陸国那賀郡 大井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列した。中世には「太郎明神」と称し、福田、飯田、石寺、寺崎、日沢の五村の総鎮守とされた。また、大井、箱田、石井、来栖の四所のうち、当社をその筆頭として一の宮明神（四所一宮）と崇敬された。 == 境内 == 本殿は、[[明和]]6年（[[1769年]]）の造営。御神木は三本の杉が根元で結ばれており、親子杉と呼ばれている。 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|親子杉（御神木） == 祭事 == *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *春祭（[[1月7日]]） *節分祭（[[2月3日]]） *御田植祭（[[7月19日]]） *秋祭（[[11月12日]]） == 参考文献 == *社頭説明板 *由緒書 == 関連項目 == * [[仲国造]] * [[大井神社 (水戸市)|大井神社]] （茨城県水戸市）- 同じく式内社「那賀郡 大井神社」の論社。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいしんしやかさまし}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 大井神社 |画像=[[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[笠間市]]大渕字輪台1652 |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 24 | 緯度秒 = 4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 16 | 経度秒 = 13 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = [[神八井耳命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 太郎明神、一の宮明神（四所一宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[11月12日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Ibaraki }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
鳥居
]] '''大井神社'''（おおいじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[笠間市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *'''[[神八井耳命]]''' === 祭神について === 祭神の神八井耳命は、初代[[神武天皇]]の皇子で、二代[[綏靖天皇]]の兄にあたる人物である。『[[日本書紀]]』では、神八井耳命について多臣（[[多氏]]）の祖と記され、『[[古事記]]』では、意富臣・小子部連・坂合部連・火君・大分君・阿蘇君・筑紫三家連・雀部臣・雀部造・小長谷造・都祁直・伊余国造・科野国造・道奥石城国造・常道仲国造・長狭国造・伊勢船木直・尾張丹羽臣・嶋田臣ら19氏の祖と記される。 このうち、当社付近は[[仲国造|常道仲国造]]が治めた地域とされており、仲国造が祖先にあたる神八井耳命を祀ったものと考えられる。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[大同]]元年（[[806年]]）に勅宣により常陸国那珂郡大井村和体ケ峯に社殿を造営し、大井大明神と称したとする。 === 概史 === 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「常陸国那賀郡 大井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列した。中世には「太郎明神」と称し、福田、飯田、石寺、寺崎、日沢の五村の総鎮守とされた。また、大井、箱田、石井、来栖の四所のうち、当社をその筆頭として一の宮明神（四所一宮）と崇敬された。 == 境内 == 本殿は[[明和]]6年（[[1769年]]）の造営。御神木は三本の杉が根元で結ばれており、親子杉と呼ばれている。 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|親子杉（御神木） == 祭事 == *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *春祭（[[1月7日]]） *節分祭（[[2月3日]]） *御田植祭（[[7月19日]]） *秋祭（[[11月12日]]） == 参考文献 == *社頭説明板 *由緒書 == 関連項目 == * [[仲国造]] * [[大井神社 (水戸市)|大井神社]] （茨城県水戸市）- 同じく式内社「那賀郡 大井神社」の論社。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいしんしやかさまし}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧村社]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 太郎明神、一の宮明神（四所一宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[11月12日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Ibaraki }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
鳥居
]] '''大井神社'''（おおいじんじゃ）は、[[茨城県]][[笠間市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *'''[[神八井耳命]]''' === 祭神について === 祭神の神八井耳命は、初代[[神武天皇]]の皇子で、二代[[綏靖天皇]]の兄にあたる人物である。『[[日本書紀]]』では、神八井耳命について多臣（[[多氏]]）の祖と記され、『[[古事記]]』では、意富臣・小子部連・坂合部連・火君・大分君・阿蘇君・筑紫三家連・雀部臣・雀部造・小長谷造・都祁直・伊余国造・科野国造・道奥石城国造・常道仲国造・長狭国造・伊勢船木直・尾張丹羽臣・嶋田臣ら19氏の祖と記される。 このうち、当社付近は[[仲国造|常道仲国造]]が治めた地域とされており、仲国造が祖先にあたる神八井耳命を祀ったものと考えられる。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）に勅宣により常陸国那珂郡大井村和体ケ峯に社殿を造営し、大井大明神と称したとする。 === 概史 === 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「常陸国那賀郡 大井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列した。中世には「太郎明神」と称し、福田、飯田、石寺、寺崎、日沢の五村の総鎮守とされた。また、大井、箱田、石井、来栖の四所のうち、当社をその筆頭として一の宮明神（四所一宮）と崇敬された。 == 境内 == 本殿は[[明和]]6年（[[1769年]]）の造営。御神木は三本の杉が根元で結ばれており、親子杉と呼ばれている。 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|親子杉（御神木） == 祭事 == *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *春祭（[[1月7日]]） *節分祭（[[2月3日]]） *御田植祭（[[7月19日]]） *秋祭（[[11月12日]]） == 参考文献 == *社頭説明板 *由緒書 == 関連項目 == * [[仲国造]] * [[大井神社 (水戸市)|大井神社]] （茨城県水戸市）- 同じく式内社「那賀郡 大井神社」の論社。 {{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいしんしやかさまし}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社]]

revision.text:
'''Ōi Shrine''' (大井神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Ōi" located in various places across Japan.
== Ōi Shrines List == * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}}（[[Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki]] [[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]]） - A Shikinaisha (式内社) candidate shrine. The enshrined deity Kashima-no-Mikoto (建借馬命) had the former name Ōfu-no-Omi (意富臣), which later became "Ōi" through phonetic changes. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kasama)|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)}}（Ibaraki Prefecture [[Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. * {{ill|Oi Shrine|ja|大井神社 (島田市)}}（[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]]） - Kokushi Genzaisha (国史見在社) and Beppyo Shrine (別表神社). Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|ja|大井神社 (名古屋市)}}（[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|ja|大井神社 (愛西市)}}（Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|ja|大井神社 (北方町)}}（[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|大井神社 (京都市)}}（[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|大井神社 (亀岡市)}}（Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha.
== Related Topics == * [[Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]]

Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|大井神社 (亀岡市)}}（Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha.

Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]]（Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha.

* changes. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kasama)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]]（Ibaraki Prefecture [[Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. * {{ill|Oi Shrine|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]]（[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]]） - Kokushi Genzaisha (国史見在社) and Beppyo Shrine (別表神社). Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]]（[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]]（Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]]（[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]]（[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]]（Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha.

* changes. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kasama)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]]（Ibaraki Prefecture [[Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. * {{ill|Oi Shrine|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]]（[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]]） - Kokushi Genzaisha (国史見在社) and Beppyo Shrine (別表神社). Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]]（[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]]（Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]]（[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]]（[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en}}[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C_categories]]<!--Ōi Shrine (Kasama)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]]（Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|qid=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * changes. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kasama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kasama)|qq=}} （Ibaraki Prefecture [[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama City]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. * {{ill|Oi Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Shrine|qq=}} （[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]]） - Kokushi Genzaisha (国史見在社) and Beppyo Shrine (別表神社). Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|qq=}} （[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|qq=}} （Aichi Prefecture [[:en:Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|qq=}} （[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[:en:Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[:en:Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} （[[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|qq=}} （Kyoto Prefecture [[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|qid=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}}

The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}}

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|qid=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}}

The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}}

Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|qq=}} （[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|qq=}} （Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|qq=}} （[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} （[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|qq=}} （Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha. == See Also == * [[Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{Disambiguation]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Oi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}} [[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[en:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:笠間市の建築物]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Oi-jinja (Kasama)]][[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|WD=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|qq=}} （[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|qq=}} （Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|qq=}} （[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} （[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|qq=}} （Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha. == See Also == * [[Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{Disambiguation]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Oi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}} [[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[en:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:笠間市の建築物]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oi-jinja (Kasama)]][[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|WD=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|qq=}} （[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|qq=}} （Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|qq=}} （[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} （[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. Named after the [[Ōigawa (Kyoto)|Ōi River (Kyoto)]]. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kameoka)|qq=}} （Kyoto Prefecture [[Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]） - Shikinaisha. == See Also == * [[Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{Disambiguation]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Oi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)}} [[sn:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[en:Ōi Shrine (Kasama)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:笠間市の建築物]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oi-jinja (Kasama)]][[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|WD=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kasama, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasama, Ibaraki]] == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasama City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Oi-jinja (Kasama)]][[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|WD=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]

Famous for the [[Shimada Obi Festival|Shimada no Obi Matsuri]]. There are several other shrines with the same name along the [[Ōi River (Shizuoka)|Ōi River]] basin. * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Nagoya)|qq=}} （[[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]] [[Nagoya|Nagoya City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Aisai)|qq=}} （Aichi Prefecture [[Aisai, Aichi|Aisai City]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kitagata)|qq=}} （[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]] [[Kitagata, Gifu|Kitagata Town]] [[Motosu District, Gifu|Motosu District]]） * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} （[[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō Ward]]） - Shikinaisha candidate shrine. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi Haiden |Location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}{{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}} Ōbuchi Aza Wadain 1652 |LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 24 | LatitudeSec = 4 | N/S = N |LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 13 | E/W = E |Deity = {{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}} |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) |Honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |OtherNames = Tarō Myōjin, Ichinomiya Myōjin (Shisho Ichinomiya) |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Ibaraki }} {{coord list}} [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Ōi Shrine''' (おおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|theoretical shrine|ja|論社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deity == * '''{{ill|Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''' === About the enshrined deity === The enshrined deity, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto, was a son of the first {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} and elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto was the ancestor of the {{ill|Tomo clan|ja|多氏}} (descendants of Ōtomo). The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} records him as the ancestor of nineteen clans, including the Ōomi, the Oko-be no Muraji, Sakaai-be no Muraji, Hi no Kimi, Ōita no Kimi, Aso no Kimi, Chikushi Mikeno Muraji, Suzume no Omi, Suzume no Miyatsuko, Onagaya no Miyatsuko, Tsuge no Naori, Iyokuni no Miyatsuko, Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Michinoku Ishijō no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Jōdō Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ise Funaki no Naori, Owari Niwa no Omi, and Shimada no Omi. Among these, the area near this shrine is thought to have been governed by the {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} (Lord of Nakakuni), and it is believed that the shrine was established by the Nakakuni clan to worship their ancestor, Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was built in {{ill|Daidō (Japan)|ja|大同}} 1 ({{ill|806|ja|806年}}) by imperial decree at Watai-ga-mine in Ōi Village, Naka District, Hitachi Province, and was called Ōi Daimyōjin. === Outline of history === In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is listed as “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province,” included among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. In the Middle Ages, it was called “Tarō Myōjin” and revered as the general guardian shrine of the five villages of Fukuda, Iida, Ishidera, Terasaki, and Hizawa. Among the four shrines of Ōi, Hakoda, Ishii, and Kurusu, this shrine was regarded as the foremost and worshiped as “Ichinomiya Myōjin” (Shisho Ichinomiya). == Grounds == The main hall (honden) was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和}} 6 ({{ill|1769|ja|1769年}}). The sacred tree is a group of three cedar trees joined at the base, known as the “Parent and Child Cedars.” File:Oi Jinja Kasama Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Honden) File:Oi Jinja Kasama Oyakosugi.jpg|Parent and Child Cedars (Sacred tree) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}}) * Rice-Planting Festival ({{ill|July 19|ja|7月19日}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|November 12|ja|11月12日}}) == References == * Shrine information board * History brochure == See also == * {{ill|Nakakuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}} * {{ill|Ōi Shrine (Mito, Ibaraki)|ja|大井神社 (水戸市)}} – another shrine regarded as a theoretical candidate for the “Ōi Shrine, Naka District, Hitachi Province” of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. {{Shinto footer}}{{Ibaraki shrines list index}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Jinja Kasama}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ōi (disambiguation)|Ōi (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Ōimachi (disambiguation)|Ōimachi (disambiguation)]] * [[Ōi River (disambiguation)]] {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (笠間市)|version=91161247|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (愛西市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (北方町)]] [[ja:大井神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:大井神社 (亀岡市)]] [[ja:大井神社]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Kasama)}} {{wikidata link|Q66564749}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Oi-jinja (Kasama)]][[ja:大井神社 (笠間市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kasama|WD=Q845851}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.40111111111111|N|140.27027777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q66564749]] Wikidata name: Oi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ōi Shrine |Image = [[File:Oi Jinja Kasama Haiden.jpg|270px]]
